title: Glass Table NOC Display
spl:
| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/ui/views
| search label="Glass*" OR label="NOC*"
| table title label author updated
| sort -updated
description: Real-time service visualization for operations centers provides at-a-glance awareness of infrastructure and service health.
value: Real-time service visualization for operations centers provides at-a-glance awareness of infrastructure and service health.
implementation: Design Glass Tables representing logical infrastructure views (network topology, service dependency map, data center layout). Map ITSI services and KPIs to visual elements. Deploy on NOC screens with auto-refresh.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk ITSI Glass Tables.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: ITSI service/KPI data.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Design Glass Tables representing logical infrastructure views (network topology, service dependency map, data center layout). Map ITSI services and KPIs to visual elements. Deploy on NOC screens with auto-refresh.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/ui/views
| search label="Glass*" OR label="NOC*"
| table title label author updated
| sort -updated
```

Understanding this SPL

**Glass Table NOC Display** — Real-time service visualization for operations centers provides at-a-glance awareness of infrastructure and service health.

Documented **Data sources**: ITSI service/KPI data. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk ITSI Glass Tables. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Calls Splunk `rest` to read configuration or REST-exposed entities.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• Pipeline stage (see **Glass Table NOC Display**): table title label author updated
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: ITSI Glass Table (custom visual layout with service health indicators, KPI widgets, and status icons).
visualization: ITSI Glass Table (custom visual layout with service health indicators, KPI widgets, and status icons).
